response:
The project is divided into 5 phases with man-hours following a geometric progression. The common ratio is 2. Let the first phase require x man-hours. The total man-hours for the project is the sum of the geometric series: x + 2x + 4x + 8x + 16x = 31x. The total man-hours required is 310. Therefore 31x = 310. Solving for x gives x = 10. The man-hours required for each phase are 10, 20, 40, 80, and 160.

The initial timeline for the project was 20 weeks. Each phase will now take 1.5 times longer. The total project duration is calculated by multiplying the initial duration by 1.5. The new total project duration is 30 weeks. The increase in time does not affect the order of the phases.

The initial plan was based on a structured approach. The project manager ensured that all teams were aligned with the timeline. The strict schedule allowed for efficient resource allocation. The phases were carefully planned to avoid delays. The teams followed the schedule with minimal disruptions. The project manager monitored progress closely. The timeline was adjusted as needed. The resource allocation plan was flexible. The teams were trained to meet the deadlines. The project manager ensured that all tasks were completed on time. The project manager reviewed the progress regularly. The project manager made adjustments as necessary. The project manager ensured that all phases were completed as planned. The project manager focused on maintaining the timeline. The project manager ensured that the project was completed within the revised schedule. The project manager made sure that all teams were aware of the changes. The project manager ensured that the project was completed successfully. The fortune of the project manager was evident in the successful completion.
